page: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing remote Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across multi-core and memory bandwidth tests. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14505.83 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6048.0, both falling in the average tier. Memory read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks reached 565.69 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths were measured at 94.59 GB/sec and 48.87 GB/sec, respectively. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1092.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 6214.28.

Given its hardware profile, the D8pls_v5 is suited for scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores but do not require large memory footprints, such as static web serving, where it demonstrated a throughput of 10.24 GB/sec, or Redis database operations, processing 11.2 million SET operations per second. The lack of x86-specific vector extensions and weak single-threaded performance make it less suitable for heavy cryptographic operations, complex image processing, or large-scale LLM inference. The instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-native development, CI/CD pipelines, and lightweight containerized microservices.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance from the DPLSv5 family, powered by an 8-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6048.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 14505.83, alongside average memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1092.0. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for scale-out, memory-lean workloads such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and ARM-native application development, but is less suited for memory-intensive databases or complex cryptographic tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage support, optimized for memory-lean scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v5 is an ARM-based VM with 8 dedicated Ampere cores and 16.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-lean scale-out workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine with dedicated physical cores and a low memory footprint, optimized for scale-out workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
No local storage with support for remote Premium Storage
Average multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving
Best suited for ARM-native development, Redis, and lightweight microservices
